questions.prompt: It's your first morning. You're finally here. You...

feedback.q1b: Early bird gets the good light.

answers.label: Need that quiet coffee moment before the world starts
Are already dressed. You were up before your alarm
Are in bed googling 'best breakfast near me' (priorities)
Text the group: 'what's the plan?'

questions.prompt: You have one week off. No obligations. Where does your mind go first?

answers.label: Somewhere I've never been. A place that feels completely different from home.
Back to a place I loved. Why risk it on somewhere new?
Honestly? Nowhere. A week at home with nothing planned sounds perfect.
Somewhere with a list. Places I need to see before I die.
Wherever my people are going. The destination matters less than the company.

questions.prompt: The hotel you want is $150/night more than the one down the street. You...

answers.label: Book the cheaper one. That's four nice dinners.
Check the reviews obsessively. If it's actually better, maybe.
Book the nicer one. Where you sleep matters.
$150 difference? I'm looking at the wrong hotels entirely.

questions.prompt: It's 2pm on day 3 of your trip. You're tired but there's a famous market that closes in an hour. You...

answers.label: Find a café and people-watch for an hour. No rush.
Push through. I'll sleep when I'm dead.
Coffee, then see what happens.
Head back to the hotel for a proper nap.

questions.prompt: You see a long line outside a famous restaurant. You...

answers.label: Get in line. It's famous for a reason.
Keep walking. Life's too short to wait.
Google if there's a reservation hack or better time.
Wonder what the locals eat instead.

questions.prompt: A friend invites you on a group trip. Eight people, shared house, one week. Your honest reaction?

answers.label: I'm in. Group trips are the best. Shared chaos, inside jokes, someone else handles logistics.
Depends entirely on the eight people. Right group, amazing. Wrong group, nightmare.
I'd rather do a smaller trip with 2-3 of them. Eight is a lot.
Probably not. I love those people, but I need more control over my own time.
I'll pass. I travel to get away from social obligations, not create new ones.

questions.prompt: You have one full day in a city you've never visited. No obligations. What actually drives your choices?

feedback.q7d: You know what you're here for.

answers.label: Art. If there's a great museum, that's where I'll be. I could spend the whole day in one gallery.
Food. Every meal is researched. I'll see things between reservations.
Photography. I've scouted the best spots and timed visits for good light. The photos matter.
A specific interest: architecture, sports venues, music history, something niche. I know what I'm looking for.
Wandering. I'll find what I find. The city reveals itself.
The highlights. I want to see what this place is famous for.
Something active. A hike, a bike tour, getting outside the city center.

questions.prompt: You're booking a place to stay. Be honest. What actually matters most to you?

answers.label: Location. I'll sacrifice space and amenities to be in the right neighborhood.
The bed. I need to sleep well or the whole trip suffers.
A kitchen. I want to cook at least some meals and feel like I live there.
Something interesting. I'd rather stay somewhere weird and memorable than comfortable and forgettable.
Reviews. Specifically the photos from real guests, not the listing photos.
Honestly? I don't care that much. Clean, safe, convenient. Done.

questions.prompt: You're planning a trip. Weather forecast shows rain the whole week. You...

answers.label: Change the dates. I'm not paying to be rained on.
Go anyway. Rain doesn't ruin a trip. Pack layers and find covered things to do.
Change the destination. Somewhere the weather's better.
Depends on the destination. Paris in the rain? Fine. Beach resort in the rain? No.
Rain makes places interesting. Fewer tourists. Moody photos. I kind of love it.

questions.prompt: Be honest. What's actually true about why you travel?

answers.label: I need to escape. My regular life is fine, but I need breaks from it to stay sane.
I feel most like myself when I'm somewhere new. Travel makes me who I am.
I want to see things before they're gone, or before I am. Time is short.
I want to eat and drink things I can't get at home. That's honestly most of it.
I want to be with people I love somewhere that isn't home. New places make old relationships feel new.
I want to understand the world. Every place has stories and I want to learn them.
I don't fully know. I just know I need to go.

questions.prompt: You just got home. The trip was a success if...

answers.label: I'm exhausted but happy. I did everything. No regrets.
I'm actually rested. I came back better than I left.
I have stories. Things happened that I'll be telling for years.
I feel closer to the people I traveled with. We have shared memories now.
I see the world differently. Something shifted in how I think.
I'm already planning the next one.

questions.prompt: You're walking through a market in a country where you don't speak the language. You...

answers.label: Point, smile, use hand gestures. This is the best part of travel.
Have a translation app open and ready. I want to actually communicate and understand.
Stick to stalls where someone speaks English. I'll engage, but on my terms.
I have a guide with me. They translate, explain context, and help me understand what I'm seeing.
Walk through and absorb the atmosphere. I'm here to observe, not necessarily interact.

questions.prompt: You can upgrade one thing on your next trip. What do you choose?

answers.label: The flight. I'll rough it at the hotel, but I want space and sleep on the plane.
The hotel. I don't care how I get there, but I want to sleep well every night.
The experiences. Skip-the-line, private tours, tables at restaurants I couldn't get otherwise.
The length. I'd rather have a longer trip than a fancier one.
Nothing. I'll take the savings and put them toward another trip.

questions.prompt: Which travel moment would be most meaningful to you?

answers.label: A conversation with a local that shifts your perspective.
Standing in a place you've dreamed about since childhood.
A physical challenge you weren't sure you could complete.
A perfect, unhurried meal with the people you love.
A moment of complete solitude in a beautiful place.

questions.prompt: How do you feel about meeting new people while traveling?

answers.label: I recharge in solitude. Other travelers often drain me.
Small, meaningful connections are enough. Depth over breadth.
I enjoy casual connections. Hostel chats, tour group banter, easy conversation.
The more the merrier. I love meeting everyone. Some of my best friends started as strangers on trips.

questions.prompt: Your ideal 'getting away from it all' means...

answers.label: Mountains, forests, somewhere I can't get cell service. Real nature, not a manicured park.
A different city. New streets, new cafés, new everything. Cities are my wilderness.
A beach. Sun, water, sand, simplicity.
Somewhere remote and quiet, but comfortable. Nature with good sheets.
I don't really need to 'get away.' I like my regular life.

questions.prompt: When you picture your ideal trip, who's there?

answers.label: Just me. Solo travel is when I'm most myself.
My partner, and romance is the point. Intimate dinners, beautiful settings, reconnection.
My partner, as adventure buddies, not a romantic escape.
My kids. Showing them the world is the whole point.
Close friends or family. The right people make any trip better.
Whoever wants to come. I'm flexible on companions.

questions.prompt: What role does personal wellbeing play in your travel?

answers.label: Central. I do retreats, spa programs, structured wellness. That's often the point of the trip.
Spiritual. I travel for inner work: meditation, contemplation, maybe something I can't fully articulate.
Rest. No alarm, no schedule, no obligations. Just recovery.
Physical. Moving my body is how I recharge: hiking, swimming, staying active.
Healing. Travel has been medicine for me during difficult times in my life.
Not really a factor. I travel for experiences and adventure, not restoration.

questions.prompt: Where are you in life right now? (This helps us calibrate.)

answers.label: Early days. Building my career, watching my budget, but hungry for experiences.
In the thick of it. Career, maybe family, lots of obligations. Travel is how I breathe.
Established. I know what I like. I've earned the right to travel exactly how I want.
Freer now. Fewer obligations, more time. Ready to see what I've been putting off.
Somewhere in between — I don't fit neatly into one stage.

questions.prompt: When you spend more on travel, what's the real reason?

answers.label: I want the best. Quality matters to me intrinsically. Not to impress anyone, just because it's better.
I want access. Experiences that aren't available to everyone. Skip the line, private tours, exclusive tables.
I want comfort. After working hard, I deserve to be taken care of.
I want time. A longer trip matters more than a fancier one.
I don't really splurge. I'd rather save for more trips.

questions.prompt: How much do your values shape your travel decisions?

answers.label: A lot. I research environmental impact, local ownership, and whether my money helps or harms the community.
Some. I choose local restaurants over chains and try to be respectful, but I'm not rigid about it.
I appreciate authenticity. Real experiences over tourist traps. But I don't stress about the ethics of every choice.
Honestly, not much. I'm there to enjoy myself, not to make a statement.

bucket_list.description: Not driven by list (0) to Checking off (1)

healing_focus.description: Not relevant (0) to Travel as medicine (1)

learning_focus.description: Not driven by learning (0) to Primary (1)

ethics_focus.description: Not a factor (0) to Drives decisions (1)
